The conversation covers applications in flood forecasting, medical AI with MedGemma, AI tools for scientific discovery including Co-Scientist and ERA, and emerging capabilities like speculative decoding and generative UI.

videos.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on building AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit, Model Context Protocol, and Looker. Covers MCP configuration, tool definitions, API credential setup, and creating agents that translate prompts into data requests via Looker.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
videos.summary: A YouTube automation system combining Claude Code Fable 5, Higsfield CLI, and Auphonic to create and edit video content at low cost. The pipeline involves reverse-engineering successful channels, writing scripts, recording voice-overs, and using Claude to automatically edit videos together into finished YouTube content.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS Studio is an internal workspace enabling non-technical users to query business data sources like Snowflake, Linear, and Notion using natural language. The system generates reusable JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, ensuring subsequent runs are deterministic and cost-efficient. Reliability is achieved through preflight schema injection, schema grounding rules, and query validation.

videos.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
videos.summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses scaling coding agents with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge. The conversation covers running many agents in parallel, enabling agent-to-agent communication and review, and building coordinated agent systems rather than just task parallelization.

videos.title: Meta' Secret Plan To Kill The Smartphone — With Alex Himel
videos.summary: Meta's VP of Wearables argues that AI-powered smart glasses could become the next major computing platform, potentially replacing smartphones. The discussion covers AI assistants for daily tasks, on-device AI, facial recognition privacy concerns, and agentic AI applications in wearables, with competitive context from OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon.

videos.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

videos.title: 1,000 AI agents, 0 LLM calls?
videos.summary: A tutorial on optimizing AI agent architectures to run at scale with minimal LLM calls by combining deterministic logic with agent decision-making. It covers when to use LLMs versus deterministic algorithms, callback hooks to bypass model calls, and route planning using the Spine and Sprout algorithm, demonstrating 1,000 agents running with zero LLM invocations.

videos.title: 21 Hermes Lessons to Get You Started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 lessons or use cases for Hermes, a language model. The content is educational guidance on getting started with the model.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tool calls instead of DOM parsing and screenshot analysis. It provides declarative and imperative APIs for exposing named, typed actions that agents can invoke directly.

videos.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol is a standard enabling AI agents and applications to communicate with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common architecture. The protocol uses JSON-RPC and supports both local and remote servers via STDIO or HTTP, with implementations in frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that scaling parallel AI agents for development work does not solve developer burnout because human attention remains the hard constraint. He proposes a four-layer architecture including signal agents to reduce context switching, verification gates for safety, weekly introspection loops on conversation logs, and biometric feedback integration to make agent-assisted workflows sustainable.

videos.title: Gemini Omni's Best Feature
videos.summary: Google's Gemini Omni can add objects to existing video footage by accepting text, image, or audio descriptions and automatically handling lighting and shading. It enables real-time post-production-style object insertion into existing clips.

videos.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
videos.summary: Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq, discusses his hardware chip designed for LLM inference and its $20 billion strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The conversation covers LPU vs. GPU tradeoffs, static scheduling, mixture-of-experts models, and the economic dynamics driving compute demand growth.

videos.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
videos.summary: IBM Technology discusses S.A.T.A.N, an AI system that detects zero-day vulnerabilities faster than traditional methods. The video explains how AI-driven vulnerability scanning impacts cybersecurity risk and informs defense strategies through DevSecOps integration.

videos.title: THE $1 CODER: This CODER COSTS $1 AND GIVES YOU $50 WORTH USAGE!
videos.summary: Command Code is a coding agent harness optimized for open-source models including DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, Qwen, and MiniMax. The harness improves agent performance through routing, caching, context handling, and tool-call repair, with a Go plan starting at one dollar per month. Features include slash commands, planning mode, headless mode, checkpoints, PR review, MCP support, and IDE integration.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series of models in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and MTP techniques for improved performance.

videos.title: Kiro in Jupyter Notebooks
videos.summary: Kiro is integrated into SageMaker using ACP to enable data scientists, ML engineers, and developers to work directly in Jupyter notebooks. The tool applies across multiple user types engaged in notebook-based experimentation.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
videos.summary: AI Search reviews Claude Fable 5 (also referred to as Claude Mythos 5), demonstrating its capabilities across tasks like ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, music composition, and cancer classification. The video compares Fable 5 against GPT 5.5 and discusses benchmarks and model specifications.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables rapid development and production deployment of AI agents. It provides sandboxed code execution, agent identity management, and full audit trails for production-grade agent applications.

videos.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video editing app in one hour by feeding Claude Fable 5 only screenshots of a SaaS landing page via Claude Code. The model successfully generated functional code including filler removal, clip detection, and subtitle export, though Fable 5 consumes API limits 2x faster than Opus and will be discontinued on June 22.

videos.title: 3 rules to keeping your code healthy!
videos.summary: Jasmyne Roberts outlines three rules for maintaining healthy code, emphasizing that good identifier naming depends on context rather than length. The video provides guidance on code quality principles for developers.

videos.title: How a perfectly even universe grew galaxies - Adam Brown
videos.summary: Adam Brown discusses cosmological structure formation and galaxy growth from initially uniform conditions. The interview explores fundamental physics rather than artificial intelligence methods or applications.

videos.title: Build a calendar app in AI Studio in 6 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates building a no-code calendar app using Google AI Studio and Gemini. The app extracts travel itineraries and converts them into calendar invitations using advanced reasoning capabilities.

videos.title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
videos.summary: David Ondrej recommends against using Fable 5 in Claude and suggests an alternative approach instead. The video is positioned as practical guidance for coding with AI.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford demonstrates that smaller models trained with reinforcement learning to exhibit correct tool-use behavior (like proper table querying and error correction) outperform much larger models on structured query tasks. A 4B model finetuned on Snorkel's framework showed measurable gains on FinQA benchmarks with minimal training cost.

videos.title: The Problem Solvers | Michael Truell at Cursor
videos.summary: Cursor is an AI-native coding platform that helps engineers and builders ship quality code through agents. Founded by Michael Truell, it has grown from 15 people to 700 and serves over 60% of Fortune 500 companies while collaborating closely with Anthropic.

videos.title: Stop applying AI to the old ways of working!
videos.summary: Andrew Macvean from Google for Developers argues that organizations should not simply apply AI to existing workflows, but instead rethink processes holistically for AI. He contends coding was never the true bottleneck and advocates for evolving entire lifecycles to integrate AI meaningfully.

videos.title: New AI attack reality💥
videos.summary: University of Toronto researchers created an AI worm, raising concerns about AI security vulnerabilities. The video presents a perspective on adapting to AI threats rather than preventing them, framed as a "reality check" on the evolving threat landscape.

videos.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
videos.summary: PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) is a Life OS built on Claude Code that provides AI agents with persistent memory of project architecture, coding standards, and workflows across sessions. It includes custom skills, structured workflows, a Pulse dashboard, and a seven-phase algorithm for AI-guided development.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves competitive quality on LM Arena while running on a single GPU, compared to competitors requiring four or five GPUs. The model runs on phones and can be deployed on local hardware like M4 Macs. Google shifted Gemma from a custom license to Apache 2.0, enabling sovereign nations to adopt it without lengthy procurement delays.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Design – Full AI Design Workflow 2026
videos.summary: Claude Design is an AI design tool by Anthropic that automates design workflows by generating branded landing pages and design assets from design system specifications. The tutorial demonstrates a four-step framework for building design systems and deploying production-ready outputs, positioning Claude Design as a Figma alternative.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 (TESTED): UHM... It's actually not worth it..
videos.summary: AICodeKing tests Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, finding that while Fable 5 performs well on coding and reasoning benchmarks, it does not represent a major practical improvement over existing models like Opus or GPT-5.5 in real-world use. The video evaluates safeguards, dual-use risks, and cost-efficiency, concluding that value depends on specific workflow needs.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline where monitoring signals like rage clicks, errors, and Slack messages trigger a background agent to group related issues, research code, and open pull requests. The system learns from embedding techniques and specificity to avoid random fixes and improve actionability.

videos.title: AI agents explained: Build your first agent in 8 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build autonomous multi-agent systems using the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) in Python. The tutorial covers the ReAct Framework, three agent patterns (sequential, reactive, planning), and walks through building a self-correcting Blog Writing Agent with validation and retry logic.

videos.title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast discussing social engineering attacks on AI systems, self-replicating AI worms, and nonhuman identity management. Speakers examine whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than humans and debate the severity of emerging AI security threats.

videos.title: Well, that’s a hot take on light mode for sure!
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents a debate about light mode's impact on user alertness. The speakers discuss whether the cognitive benefits of staying awake through light mode are worth the potential downsides.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class model publicly available with advanced capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, and analytics. The model includes safety systems that restrict functionality in high-risk domains like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry to limit misuse potential.

videos.title: $10,000 AI Website in 28mins
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates building a website comparable to a $10,000 professional project in just 28 minutes using AI tools. The video showcases rapid AI-assisted web development and the efficiency gains from automation.

videos.title: From prompt to a fully working real-time voice app in seconds. 🎙️✨
videos.summary: Google AI Studio enables developers to build, deploy, and remix real-time AI voice applications from natural language prompts. The tool transforms development workflows from static code to dynamic agentic systems using Gemini models.

videos.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
videos.summary: Apple revealed a rebuilt Siri with personal context, screen awareness, and Visual Intelligence at WWDC 2026, powered by Google Gemini. Apple shares fell after the keynote and the EU will not get the feature at launch. The video questions whether Apple is catching up in AI or packaging rival technology within its own ecosystem.

videos.title: Claude Mythos is FINALLY here (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos class model, which achieves top performance on benchmarks including SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code. The model demonstrates capabilities in vision, code generation, long-context reasoning, and handles various tasks from website testing to fullstack development.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 beats Pokémon FireRed only using vision
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 completed Pokémon FireRed end-to-end using only raw game screenshots and vision, without helper utilities or game-state information. Earlier Claude models required external harnesses to play the game.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job market trends in 2026 as AI becomes prevalent in hiring and resume screening. The video argues that personal networking, building a strong personal brand, and demonstrating practical skills through open source contributions and projects remain more valuable than mass applications. Junior developers are presented as still valuable despite AI advancement.

videos.title: 21 Hermes concepts to get you started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 foundational concepts related to Hermes, a model or framework. The video serves as an introductory guide for learners new to the system.

videos.title: Multiple claude agent in the same window?
videos.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which enables launching hundreds of subagents concurrently within a single session. The system automatically plans work distribution, assigns parallel tasks, and performs verification through dedicated quality assurance agents.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

videos.title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... Unlock SuperPowers
videos.summary: Claude released UltraCode, a feature that enables code-based orchestration of up to 10 parallel agents without a master LLM. The video tutorial covers implementation patterns including judge panels, adversarial verification, deterministic fan-out, and integration with OpenRouter for dynamic model selection.

videos.title: Build an app from your phone in AI Studio
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build a custom image-generation app directly from a mobile browser using Google AI Studio, voice commands, and the Nano Banana API. The tutorial shows real-time code generation without requiring software installation.

videos.title: Behind the Scenes - Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing
videos.summary: CS50 is Harvard University's foundational computer science course covering artificial intelligence, programming, and related topics. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare, with an active community across numerous social channels and discussion forums.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a GPU deployment tool that simplifies cloud inference by replacing Docker build-and-push cycles with a single @flash.endpoint decorator on async Python functions. It supports hot reload for model swaps and integrates with RunPod's serverless GPU infrastructure, enabling automatic scaling across data centers.

videos.title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
videos.summary: Military historian Sarah Paine outlines a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework distinguishing continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest from maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade. The framework is applied to understand Russia, China, and the Industrial Revolution's transformative effects on global power structures.

videos.title: Claude Mythos 5 + Fable 5 Are Here And The Numbers Are INSANE
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 (Mythos 5 with guardrails) with strong performance metrics. Fable 5 is available at no extra cost on paid subscription plans through June 22, after which access requires usage credits.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 plays Factorio
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 autonomously plays Factorio, a factory-building game, demonstrating strategic planning and autonomous decision-making. The video showcases the model building and managing an automated factory without human intervention.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 simulates the solar system and predicts a solar eclipse
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 demonstrated the ability to build a physics-based simulation of the solar system from first principles and use it to predict solar eclipses. The model derived planetary orbital motion and celestial mechanics without pre-programmed astronomical data.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 sets a fluid simulation to Beethoven
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 generated code to create a fluid simulation synchronized to an EDM remix of classical music, which Claude also produced via code generation without prior exposure to music.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 designs a 3D-printable model in a Claude-built CAD editor
videos.summary: Claude created a browser-based CAD editor that enables 3D-printable model design. The editor includes an AI copilot built by Claude Fable 5 for automated modeling assistance.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead despite social media discourse, but rather the problem was a narrow definition of retrieval. He frames RAG as iterative agent-driven search combining vector search, full text search, regex, and filters, contrasting approaches like Cursor's upfront indexing with Claude Code's per-session grep model, and proposes embeddings as cached compute.

videos.title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
videos.summary: A Google Cloud livestream demonstrating GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening using cuML and Google Cloud's Agent Platform. The session showed 20x-45x training speedups for molecular binding predictions using cuDF and cuML on GPU hardware, enabling screening at trillion molecule scales without rewriting pandas or scikit-learn code.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Thor Schaeff demonstrates Gemini's audio stack including speaker identification, emotion detection, and language detection via a single API call; Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a real-time multimodal sound-to-sound model with integrated thinking; and Lyria 3, a music generation model capable of producing full songs with lyrics using tool use for on-demand generation.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Use Karpathy's Method Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese describes a three-layer method attributed to Andrej Karpathy for building with Claude more effectively. The approach emphasizes proper use of Claude Code and Claude Cowork to accelerate development beyond standard prompting techniques.

videos.title: How To Design a Complete Brand Identity With AI (Best AI Logo Maker)
videos.summary: Design.com is a design platform that uses AI to generate logos, brand kits, and marketing materials like business cards and social posts. The platform includes AI logo generation, one-sentence text-based editing, brand kit auto-application across templates, background removal, and animated logo generation for social media.

videos.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model supporting real-time multilingual translation across 70+ languages. The model enables live dubbing of video content, automatic language switching, and natural-sounding output, available to developers via the Gemini API and in products like Google Translate and Google Meet.

videos.title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
videos.summary: Kimi Code, powered by Kimi K2.6, enables video reasoning tasks by accepting reference videos and generating color grading LUT files. Users can query visual properties like colors, shots, and style from uploaded videos to produce production-ready outputs.

videos.title: How I'd Learn AI From Scratch in 2026 (skip the useless 80%)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents a prioritized learning strategy for AI in 2026 centered on picking one model to master, building practical context through real projects, and assembling AI systems with tools. The guidance dismisses rote prompting frameworks in favor of deeper model understanding and contextual work.

videos.title: Finally… A NotebookLM Alternative With an API
videos.summary: Open Notebook is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to Google NotebookLM that allows developers to manage documents, code, research, and AI workflows with an API. It supports features like grounded question-answering over custom sources and podcast generation from documents.

videos.title: This is why my AI Agents never guess
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses why their AI agents are designed to avoid guessing or uncertainty in their responses. The video appears to promote a principle of deterministic or reliable AI agent behavior.

videos.title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
videos.summary: Sam Anthony discusses why scaling agentic AI systems increases cost, latency, and failure risk. The video explores how multi-agent design, coordination, and system architecture affect real-world scalability of AI agents.

videos.title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
videos.summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.

videos.title: Frontier Code (GPT-5.6 VS Mythos): This BENCHMARK is ACTUALLY REAL!
videos.summary: Cognition released FrontierCode, a benchmark that measures whether AI-generated code is mergeable and production-ready by evaluating real pull request quality against maintainer-defined rubrics. It covers code from 36 open-source repositories across multiple programming languages and reports lower false positive rates than prior benchmarks like SWE-Bench Pro.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
videos.summary: Xavier Plantaz demonstrates running Gemma 4 E2B on Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson Orin Nano using LiteRT for on-device inference. The system uses Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for multimodal inference, and Kokoro for text-to-speech to enable real-time conversational interaction with robotic hardware.

videos.title: New BEST local AI image generator is here!
videos.summary: Ideogram 4 is an open-source local AI image generator featuring world knowledge, text rendering, prompt adherence, bounding box control, and support for comics and manga. The video demonstrates integration with ComfyUI and includes benchmarks comparing its performance and license terms.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: AI Engineer is promoting the World's Fair event in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026. Tickets and schedule information are available at ai.engineer/wf.

videos.title: Graphify + Obsidian + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
videos.summary: Chase AI demonstrates a workflow combining Graphify for repository analysis, Obsidian for knowledge organization, and Claude Code for improved code generation. The approach integrates repository insights into a larger knowledge vault to enhance Claude Code's outputs.

videos.title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
videos.summary: OpenAI is reportedly transforming ChatGPT from a chatbot into a full AI super app by integrating Codex, agents, image tools, third-party app support, workplace automation, and enhanced security features into a unified interface. This represents a significant platform overhaul ahead of OpenAI's reported IPO listing.

videos.title: Working Like a Lawyer with Claude
videos.summary: Mark Pike from Anthropic and Anna Gressel from Freshfields discuss how lawyers use Claude for legal work. They explore how AI is changing legal practice while maintaining that final judgment calls remain with human lawyers.

videos.title: Stop managing infrastructure for AI Agents 🛠️
videos.summary: Google announced managed Agents in the Gemini API that handle infrastructure provisioning and execution. The service runs autonomous agents in a remote Google-hosted sandbox via a single API call, reducing operational overhead for developers.

videos.title: Intro to multi-agent systems with ADK
videos.summary: Google released the Agent Development Kit (ADK), a framework designed to simplify building and testing multi-agent systems. The tutorial demonstrates creating agents with custom tools including real-time date retrieval and Google Search integration, along with a built-in web interface for debugging.

videos.title: Claude Code + Graphify = Insane Agentic OS
videos.summary: Graphify builds a knowledge graph representation of code projects, allowing Claude to understand repository structure without re-reading entire codebases. This reduces token costs, latency, and improves accuracy when working with Claude Code for agentic systems.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin from Together AI presents a stack of techniques for training LLMs with multi-million token context: fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. A novel contribution called Untied Ulysses further optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention heads and reusing buffers, enabling 25% longer sequences than prior Ulysses implementations.

videos.title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
videos.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that has been generally available for one year. It runs in the terminal and is used by developers and organizations for code generation, verification, and deployment across codebases. Recent features include auto mode, routines for CI and code review, agent view, voice mode, and Remote Control.

videos.title: AI YouTube Automation: How to Start a YouTube Channel With AI
videos.summary: AI Master is a workflow platform for automated YouTube video production covering idea validation, scripting, avatar creation, B-roll generation, editing, and publishing. The tutorial demonstrates the complete production pipeline from research through upload and repurposing, integrating tools like ChatGPT, Claude, HeyGen, Descript, and Opus Clip.

videos.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
videos.summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

videos.title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
videos.summary: A podcast discussion covering Apple's operating system AI integration announced at WWDC, Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement capabilities, and competitive dynamics between Microsoft and OpenAI. Topics include whether Apple is finally shipping AI aligned with actual use cases, concerns about Anthropic's ability to maintain ethical practices as a potential public company, and questions about whether Microsoft or OpenAI will dominate cloud AI services.

videos.title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
videos.summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an agentic platform that orchestrates specialized sub-agents for software development tasks. It unbundled from a simple IDE into a full ecosystem with a desktop agent manager, CLI, advanced SDK, and custom IDE. The platform enables developers to use voice prompts and skills to coordinate parallel agents for building, localizing, and deploying applications.

videos.title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
videos.summary: David Ondrej demonstrates Hermes Agent, an open-source agentic framework. The video discusses running Hermes Agent with MiniMax m3 models to reduce inference costs by 100x compared to alternatives.

videos.title: How A Beauty Company Built An AI Agent — With Rachel Williamson and Josh Siebert
videos.summary: Ulta Beauty deployed ServiceNow's AI-powered HR platform across a 65,000-person workforce to automate employee-facing processes. The deployment influenced hiring decisions and the company is extending AI into onboarding and employee lifecycle management.

videos.title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
videos.summary: Long context windows do not fix agent degradation in the middle of documents, a phenomenon called the U curve. Nupur Sharma presents iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self correction as solutions, along with a hybrid model approach where high-reasoning models handle discovery and lighter models perform validation.

videos.title: Kimi Code, our open-source coding agent, just got a major upgrade!
videos.summary: Kimi Code is an open-source coding agent with one-line CLI installation and zero setup. It supports video-to-code conversion, plugins for stocks and financial reports, IDE integrations via the ACP protocol with JetBrains and Zed, and hooks for custom tools.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid fans and formal customer interviews are poor signals of real product-market fit. True PMF manifests as customer pull rather than push, and founders should embed deeply in customer workflows instead of conducting point-in-time interviews. He advises betting on AI model trajectory rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
videos.summary: Cloudflare's Dynamic Workers enable safe execution of LLM-generated code in sandboxed isolates with explicit permission grants. The system avoids ambient access and is positioned as a compute primitive for AI agents. Cloudflare is shipping a coding agent harness built entirely on Workers.

videos.title: Can you spot which committed file explains this mismatch?
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents a package management puzzle where local development and CI install the same dependency but end up with different versions despite unchanged package.json. The video challenges viewers to identify which committed file explains the version mismatch by understanding how dependency resolution works.

videos.title: Headroom: The Netflix Tool That Makes AI Agents 10x Cheaper
videos.summary: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses inputs to LLMs using content-type-aware compressors and locally trained models, reducing token usage by 60-95%. Built by a Netflix engineer, it keeps compression local and uses breadcrumb hashing for on-demand retrieval of full original content.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model implementing Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention. The model includes optimizations for KV cache efficiency and tiled I/O improvements that reduce computational overhead during prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: What Is Agentic Coding? How AI Agents Modernize Code
videos.summary: Agentic coding uses AI agents to automate development tasks, helping developers understand and modernize legacy code. The approach accelerates software development workflows by reducing time spent on code comprehension and improving DevSecOps outcomes in complex systems.

videos.title: Claude Oceanus V1-P (Mythos?- FULLY TESTED): I TESTED IT & IT'S ACTUALLY CRAZY!
videos.summary: AICodeKing tested a leaked model appearing as Oceanus V1-P (possibly linked to Mythos) across coding, visual, reasoning, and game tasks using OpenCode benchmarking. The model achieved perfect scores and outperformed Opus, GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, DeepSeek V4 Pro, and other competitors in the tested domains.

videos.title: I Turned Cheap Cloud Storage Into a 1PB Local Drive (With JuiceFS)
videos.summary: JuiceFS is a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that layers cloud object storage like AWS S3 with a multi-tiered caching engine using local NVMe for performance. The video demonstrates setting up JuiceFS with Redis metadata, configuring local SSD cache, and running AI inference workloads directly against cloud buckets while monitoring performance via Prometheus and Better Stack observability.

He cites mathematician Ramanujan as an exemplar of the unmeasurable human qualities that define meaningful intelligence.

videos.title: Senior engineers don't really write code anymore
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery presents the claim that senior engineers do not write code in the conventional way most developers understand. The video promises a detailed explanation of this assertion.

videos.title: Automate your deployment pipelines
videos.summary: Kiro CLI includes a headless mode for non-interactive execution in CI/CD workflows and shell scripts. The livestream covers automating code generation, integrating Kiro into CI pipelines, and scripting multi-step development tasks.

videos.title: Build AI Apps with Claude Code  | Full Tutorial for Beginners (Desktop + Terminal + VS Code)
videos.summary: A tutorial on building agentic AI applications using Claude Code in three modes: desktop app, terminal, and VS Code integration. The example builds a Resume and Cover Letter Generator with features for personalization, skill suggestions, and improvement recommendations. Covers installation, setup, and local execution using Anthropic API.

videos.title: DeepSeek V4 Analysis..
videos.summary: DeepSeek released V4, a flagship model that achieves significant reductions in inference compute and cost. The analysis examines how V4 represents progress toward leaner and more intelligent LLMs, and discusses differing incentives between open-source and proprietary AI development approaches.

The toolkit is designed to improve agent success rates and reduce errors when deploying AWS infrastructure like Lambda functions and API Gateway endpoints.

videos.title: Amazon EKS Auto Mode | Fully Automated Kubernetes Clusters
videos.summary: Amazon EKS Auto Mode is a fully managed Kubernetes service that provisions production-grade clusters with a single click. It automates both control plane and data plane management while allowing customization for power users, and includes built-in features like Karpenter-based auto-scaling and cost optimization for AI workloads.

videos.title: How Alex Hormozi ACTUALLY Uses AI
videos.summary: Austin Marchese analyzes Alex Hormozi's AI usage approach across four pillars: data as competitive advantage, voice-first interaction, workflow decomposition, and compound effects. The video identifies a mistake where teams over-relied on AI tools without proper safeguards, requiring executive intervention to correct operational behavior.

videos.title: Getting Started with Kiro for Non-Technicals
videos.summary: Kiro is an AI-powered development platform designed to enable non-technical users to write code and automate tasks. The tutorial covers account setup, interface navigation, agentic chat capabilities, and features like MCP servers, steering files, and customizable skills.

videos.title: ZimaBoard 2 Review: Set Up Your Own AI Server (And Run OpenClaw!)
videos.summary: ZimaBoard 2 is a paperback-sized server for self-hosted AI workloads. The review demonstrates installing OpenClaw locally with Venice AI, setting up a Telegram bot for automated AI news briefings, and running a sovereign AI stack without cloud vendor lock-in.

videos.title: 🔴LIVE - What's Next for Archon - Live Roadmap Session
videos.summary: Cole Medin hosted a live session discussing the roadmap and future direction of Archon. The session covered prioritized features and development plans for the open-source project available on GitHub.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

videos.title: The Problem with AI Agents No One is Talking About | Yutori, Abhishek Das
videos.summary: Abhishek Das argues that most AI agents don't work reliably in practice, with error compounding across multi-step workflows causing failures even at high per-step accuracy. He contends that the agent industry has normalized unreliability and advocates for building agents designed for first-try reliability through rigorous testing and "proof of work" methodologies.

videos.title: Top 6 Claude Code Features to Get You Ahead of 99% of Users. (In Under 10 Minutes).
videos.summary: A tutorial demonstrating six essential Claude Code features including connectors and automation capabilities. The video positions Claude Code as the most powerful part of Claude and teaches advanced usage techniques for building and enhancing projects with AI assistance.

videos.title: 🔴LIVE - Full AI Video Generation Workflow Using Claude Code + Remotion + Archon
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a production-grade AI video generation workflow that takes a URL as input and produces a fully rendered MP4. The system uses Claude for planning and code generation, Remotion for video composition, Archon for orchestration, and Cartesia and ElevenLabs for voice, music, and sound effects.

videos.title: Robot girlfriends, recursive AI agents, full AI research, Happy Horse: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI Search presents a video roundup of major AI announcements including Happy Horse, SenseNova U1, Talkie, Grok 4.3, and Vista 4D. The segment covers emerging tools spanning robotics, multi-agent systems, video generation, and large language models. The video showcases diverse AI capabilities from computer vision to multimodal agent reasoning.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new AI coding paradigm that includes automated testing, codebase harness setup, and workflow orchestration. The video covers Symphony's mechanics, integration with testing infrastructure, and configuration through WORKFLOW.md files.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: How Two Devs used Kiro to win a Robotics Hackathon and Fund their Startup
videos.summary: Jason and Chidi built Gofer AI, a robotics platform using imitation learning, with Kiro at the LabLab AI hackathon. They won the hackathon and secured funding. Kiro enabled spec-driven development of a modular robotics pipeline including video input, motion extraction, and Isaac Sim replay.

videos.title: You Deleted Everything and AWS Is Still Charging You
videos.summary: AWS Developers video walks through a cleanup checklist for identifying surprise charges that persist after deleting cloud resources. Covers hidden costs from RDS snapshots, EBS volumes, elastic IPs, and NAT gateways, and recommends setting up billing alarms for cost monitoring.

videos.title: How I Used Claude Code to Build a $481k App
videos.summary: Austin Marchese demonstrates using Claude Code to build a product generating $481,000 annually in 45 days with zero prior coding experience. The video covers five steps for shipping fast: starting with problems, identifying what to engineer versus reuse, learning technical vocabulary, planning before building, and anticipating failure points.

videos.title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build With AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Systems Architecture App
videos.summary: A full-stack SaaS development tutorial showing architecture-first workflow with AI agents, proper context management, and deployment using Next.js, Prisma, Postgres, TypeScript, and Tailwind. Covers authentication, real-time collaboration via Liveblocks, automated workflows with Trigger.dev, and AI-driven spec generation for systems architecture design.

videos.title: This 25-Year-Old Built a 2-Year Moat Nobody Can Bet Against | Corgi, Nico Laqua & Emily Yuan
videos.summary: Nico Laqua and Emily Yuan, co-founders of Corgi insurance, secured $108M in Series A funding by rebuilding the insurance industry from first principles rather than modernizing legacy carriers. The founders emphasize pursuing ambitious versions of ideas and building defensible moats through time, energy, and relentless execution despite years of regulatory challenges.

videos.title: The insane engineering of Deepseek V4
videos.summary: DeepSeek V4 is an LLM featuring a 1M token context window and hybrid attention mechanisms including CSA, sparse selection, HCA, and sliding window attention for efficiency. The model incorporates anticipatory routing and achieves state-of-the-art results with novel training and infrastructure optimizations.

videos.title: Kiro subagents explained
videos.summary: Kiro introduces subagents in its IDE to address context window limitations in agentic development. Each subagent operates independently with its own isolated context window and scope, enabling developers to scale agent-based systems in large codebases.

videos.title: How to setup and use Claude Code for FREE  (Step-by-Step Guide)
videos.summary: Tutorial on setting up and using Claude Code with Ollama to build agentic AI applications locally without paid APIs. Covers installation, LLM selection, and construction of a complete travel agency application using Python and Flask with multiple AI agents.

videos.title: Developer spotlight: How Kiro resurrected fax machines for seniors
videos.summary: Robert Willson used Kiro to build Faxi, an AI-powered fax-to-internet bridge enabling offline seniors in Japan to access email, shopping, and online services via fax machines. The talk covers how Kiro shaped development from prototype to production.

videos.title: Google TPU 8t and 8i is here..
videos.summary: Google has released TPU 8t for training and TPU 8i for inference, which are ASIC chips served via Virgo Network. The video compares Google's TPUs against NVIDIA GPUs and analyzes how scaling strategies affect the economics of token serving and LLM deployment at scale.

videos.title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer (Build Anything with AI)
videos.summary: Cole Medin presents a three-phase agentic engineering workflow: Planning (turning ideas into PRDs and tickets automatically), the PIV Loop (Plan-Implement-Validate cycle per ticket), and System Evolution (using bugs to improve the AI layer system-wide). The methodology is designed to be lightweight and composable with existing development practices.

videos.title: AgentCore Managed Harness: Getting Started
videos.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Managed Harness simplifies agent deployment by replacing manual container and orchestration setup with a JSON config file and system prompt. It provides isolated micro VMs, persistent storage, cross-session memory, hot-swap model capabilities, and browser and code interpreter access out of the box.

videos.title: Diffusion Models explained..
videos.summary: Educational video explaining diffusion models as a process and framework rather than a fixed architecture. Covers the progression, strengths, weaknesses, and implementation details including how diffusion can be combined with transformers such as in DiT.

videos.title: How Claude Code's Creator ACTUALLY Automates his work
videos.summary: Austin Marchese breaks down how Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, uses Claude Skills for automation through agents, hooks, and loops. The video reverse-engineers Cherny's system design and identifies which Claude Code features are underutilized for productivity.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 7 - Pokémon
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 7 covers Pokémon-inspired RPG mechanics including state stacks, GUI design, and turn-based battle systems as an introduction to game programming. The lecture is part of Harvard's CS50 computer science course available through edX and other platforms. This is the final lecture in the CS50 2D sequence taught by David J. Malan.

videos.title: This 26 y/o 10x'd Revenue Doing the Opposite of Every AI Startup | Paraform, John Kim
videos.summary: John Kim built Paraform by positioning human recruiters as the core competitive advantage in hiring, contrary to industry trends toward automation. The company has paid out over $50 million to recruiters and achieved 10x revenue growth in 2025 while serving customers like Palantir and Rippling.

videos.title: My Simple Claude Cowork System (for normal people)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents Claude Cowork, a system that transforms Claude from a chatbot into a full operating system for work. The approach includes CLAUDE.md instruction files, persistent memory, a three-level hierarchy of workstations and projects, and practical demonstrations like Email HQ and spending trackers.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Cowork (Step-by-Step) | AI Agent on Your Computer
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is an AI agent that runs on desktop computers to automate daily tasks. It can interact with files, emails, calendars, and applications to complete complex workflows automatically. The tutorial demonstrates file organization, task scheduling, and email summarization capabilities.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 6 - Angry Birds
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 6 covers game physics implementation using Box2D physics engine integrated with Love2D framework. The lecture demonstrates dynamic bodies, collision detection, joints, and trajectory simulation for Angry Birds-style mobile game development.

videos.title: Claude Design Basics | Master 95% in 10 Minutes
videos.summary: Tristen O'Brien presents a 6-step framework for using Claude Design, Anthropic's new design tool. The video demonstrates building landing pages, pitch decks, and mobile app prototypes from text prompts without coding or design experience, covering interface basics through export and handoff to Claude Code.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 5 - Legend of Zelda
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 5 teaches game mechanics through the Legend of Zelda, covering dungeon generation, hitbox design, screen scrolling, and data-driven game development. The course is offered through Harvard's extension and summer programs, edX, and open courseware.

videos.title: The most insane week in AI
videos.summary: Video overview of recent AI developments across multiple domains including new model releases from DeepSeek, OpenAI, Kimi, Xiaomi, and Alibaba Qwen, alongside research projects in game engines, geometry understanding, image generation, and video editing. The content emphasizes rapid innovation velocity and breadth of AI applications from language models to robotics.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 4 - Super Mario Bros.
videos.summary: CS50 2D is Harvard's game development course that teaches platformer building with tile maps, animations, procedural level generation, collision detection, and game objects in Lua. The lecture series is available on edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

videos.title: Claude Design Tutorial: Build a Website in Minutes with AI
videos.summary: Claude Design is an Anthropic feature that generates website prototypes, landing pages, and presentations from text prompts. Users can create designs using pre-built templates or custom specifications, then export functional code for web pages.

videos.title: GPT-5.5 is a total freak
videos.summary: The AI Search channel presents a review of OpenAI's GPT-5.5, claiming it is exceptional. The video demonstrates the model's capabilities across diverse tasks including code generation, 3D rendering, cancer detection, music composition, and deep research, while also addressing hallucination issues and providing performance benchmarks.

videos.title: SQL Full Course - Build a Database and Deploy It
videos.summary: An AWS tutorial video covering foundational SQL concepts, database design, and hands-on deployment to the cloud. Covers CRUD operations, normalization, relationships, JOINs, and deploying MySQL databases to Amazon RDS.

videos.title: How to 10x Your Claude Code Projects (Karpathy's Method)
videos.summary: Austin Marchese describes three strategies attributed to Andrej Karpathy for improving productivity with Claude Code: LLM Knowledge Bases, Auto-Research, and Context Engineering. The video frames these as a system that can "10x" code project output and positions them as actionable principles for developers using Claude.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 3 - Match 3
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 3 covers building a Match-3 game using tile grids, tweening, timers, and color palettes. The lecture is part of Harvard University's CS50 introduction to computer science and programming, available through edX and Harvard Extension School.

videos.title: Most AI agents are fake (here's a 10-second test)
videos.summary: Jeff Su argues that most companies falsely label products as AI agents when they perform only classification or basic generation. He proposes a simple test: genuine agents autonomously think and iterate, while non-agentic AI merely outputs classifications, numbers, or generated text without autonomous behavior. He uses Google and Anthropic products to illustrate the distinction.

videos.title: Search Relevance Built Into the Vector Index
videos.summary: Qdrant released Query API 1.17.0 with built-in relevance feedback mechanisms for vector search. The feature enables injecting relevance signals directly into the vector index and building adaptive, feedback-driven retrieval systems beyond reranking. The implementation is demonstrated via tutorial for semantic search over documentation.

videos.title: Voice AI that transcribes and takes action
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a voice AI agent that transcribes spoken input and performs corresponding actions. The agent uses function calling to execute user requests.

videos.title: How to Make $20,000/Month with Claude Code
videos.summary: Liam Ottley argues that retainer-based AI agency models for small and medium-sized businesses are now viable and profitable due to Claude Code, enabling recurring monthly revenue starting at $2,500 and scaling to $20,000+. The model involves deploying AI Operating Systems on-site and providing ongoing management and optimization services.

videos.title: Using Hermes to automate social media posts
videos.summary: Keith AI demonstrates using Hermes to automate and systematize social media posts. The video focuses on building repeatable processes that improve post quality rather than relying on guesswork.

videos.title: Biomedical AI Copilot with Neo4j and Qdrant
videos.summary: A biomedical AI copilot demonstration integrating Neo4j graph database and Qdrant vector search to navigate PubMed literature. The system uses context engineering techniques to wire retrieval-augmented generation into an agentic system for medical research applications.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 2 - Breakout
videos.summary: CS50 2D is Harvard University's course on game development. The course is available through edX and Harvard Extension School.

videos.title: Best LLM model for Hermes

videos.title: Turn Any Workflow Into a One-Step “Skill”
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a method for abstracting complex workflows into single-step callable skills. This approach enables automation and reusability of multi-step processes as atomic operations.

videos.summary: Qdrant Agent Skills is a new feature that encodes real-world engineering knowledge to help AI agents optimize vector search retrieval and understand when and how to tune performance. The feature moves beyond treating vector search as a black box to enable better search quality and decision-making in production AI systems.

videos.title: Meet Kimi K2.6 Agent Swarm 🐝
videos.summary: Kimi K2.6 introduces Agent Swarm, a system that coordinates up to 300 sub-agents across 4,000 coordinated steps. The system positions multi-agent orchestration as an advancement in test-time compute efficiency.

videos.title: From App Idea to Deployed Infrastructure in minutes

videos.title: Kimi K2.6 and Agent Swarm explained..
videos.summary: Kimi K2.6 is an LLM released by MoonShot AI designed as a coding model optimized for long-running tasks and agent swarm applications. It represents maturation in Chinese AI labs' model capabilities.

videos.title: Master Hermes Agent in 41 mins

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 1 - Flappy Bird
videos.summary: CS50 2D Lecture 1 teaches game development fundamentals by walking through the recreation of Flappy Bird. The course covers sprites, parallax scrolling, procedural pipe generation, collision detection, state machines, and input handling as core concepts for 2D game development.

videos.title: New AI image generator BEATS EVERYTHING

The tool demonstrates capabilities in text rendering, animation frame generation, diagram creation, and scene composition at scale.

videos.title: This Ex-Quant Had to Quit Wall Street to Build a 24/7 Stock Exchange | QFEX, Annanay Kapila

He critiques high-frequency trading as value-destructive and describes the financial sector as extracting top talent through high compensation rather than creating meaningful work.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Lecture 0 - Pong
videos.summary: CS50 2D is Harvard's introductory course on 2D game development fundamentals using Lua and Love2D. The first lecture covers Pong, teaching game loops, input handling, collision detection, object-oriented programming, game states, and sound integration.

videos.title: How Anthropic Employees ACTUALLY Use Claude Code to Grow
videos.summary: Austin Marchese analyzes how Anthropic's Growth Team used Claude Code to achieve rapid scaling from $1 billion to $19 billion in 14 months. He identifies four strategies: automating existing workflows, enabling previously uneconomical work, building unique tools, and using AI as a thought partner, while highlighting a common ceiling where AI becomes busywork rather than leverage.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews features of agent-based AI systems including autoresearch, memory architectures, and self-evolving mechanisms. The video covers multiple agent frameworks and capabilities that enable autonomous task execution and skill development.

videos.title: Why Inference is hard..
videos.summary: A video overview of inference optimization techniques covering model loading strategies (mmap), quantization methods (standard, GGUF, AWQ, EXL2, FP8, NVFP4), and inference engines (llama.cpp, vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, TGI). The presentation addresses pre-fill, decoding, concurrency, and scheduling challenges in LLM serving.

videos.title: Meet Kimi K2.6: Advancing Open-Source Coding
videos.summary: Kimi AI announced Kimi K2.6, an advancement in open-source coding capabilities. The release emphasizes long-horizon coding tasks, coding-driven design approaches, and agent swarm functionality.

videos.title: CS50 2D - Introduction
videos.summary: CS50 2D is Harvard's introduction to 2D game development that explores the design and mechanics of classic games including Pong, Flappy Bird, Breakout, Super Mario Bros., and others. The course is available through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare platforms.

videos.title: The 6 Claude Features to Get You Ahead of 99% of People using AI
videos.summary: Tutorial covering six Claude products and features including Claude.ai, desktop app, mobile interface, and API access for building AI agents. Highlights privacy controls and data handling. Emphasizes that most users leverage only a fraction of Claude's full capabilities across writing, thinking, research, and task automation.

videos.title: Stop perfecting your prompt. Do this instead
videos.summary: Jeff Su argues that practitioners should shift focus from prompt perfection to context engineering. He contrasts grounding and RAG as techniques that anchor AI outputs to real data, and emphasizes assembling comprehensive context over optimizing wording.

videos.title: TanStack Start Full Course 2026 | Build and Deploy a Full Stack Application
videos.summary: A full-course tutorial on TanStack Start, a framework addressing hydration errors and RSC directive complexity. The course covers routing, data fetching, server functions, API routes, SEO best practices, and includes a complete full-stack marketplace application (Skild) built from scratch to deployment.

videos.title: keeping up with AI is making you fall behind (just focus on this one thing)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley argues that keeping up with AI news creates anxiety and distraction. He recommends niching down rather than chasing every release, using pattern thinking to spot trends, and focusing on AI Operating Systems as a major market opportunity.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude to Code. Do THIS Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese argues that Claude Code is not just for coding but should form the foundation of a personal AI operating system that learns user patterns, retains memory, and improves with use. He frames this as "owning" AI rather than "renting" it and positions this approach as critical for building sustainable AI-driven workflows and scaling to teams.

videos.title: Claude Code Leak: What's Happening?
videos.summary: A leaked NPM source map revealed Claude Code's internal architecture, showing it is a TypeScript harness wrapping an LLM with a 3-layer memory system. The video analyzes implications for token economics, copyright liability, and privacy telemetry, arguing that open-source alternatives reduce vendor lock-in for individual builders.

videos.title: AI Coding Tools Were Lying to You
videos.summary: Kiro CLI is a code intelligence tool that uses Language Server Protocol (LSP) to enable AI-assisted code editing with semantic understanding. It provides symbol lookup, AST-based pattern search, error detection, and safe refactoring capabilities.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

videos.title: How are you using steering, sub agents and skills?
videos.summary: Kiro explores best practices for guiding AI agents through steering docs, reusable skills, and sub-agent delegation. The video addresses when to use each approach and warns against overusing sub-agents as capabilities increase.

videos.title: Claude Routines Just Dropped, And It's Perfect
videos.summary: Claude Routines is a new feature that allows users to schedule, set up webhooks, and trigger cloud AI agent flows via API calls. The feature enables automation of tasks like email drafting and proposal generation, integrating with workflow platforms like n8n.

videos.title: What’s new in Gemma 4?
videos.summary: Gemma 4 is a new family of open models from Google DeepMind supporting advanced reasoning, native vision and audio, and agentic tool-use capabilities. The models are designed to run across a wide range of hardware from high-end workstations to mobile phones.

videos.title: how to sell $5,000 Claude Code AIOS to SMBs (full breakdown)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley and Tyler Nelson outline a bottom-up playbook for AI agencies to build and sell AI operating systems (AIOS) to small and medium-sized businesses. The approach involves creating a Context OS that integrates chatbot history and external tools like CRM and analytics, layering automations and agents, and implementing ROI tracking before productizing as turnkey solutions or managed services.

videos.title: The physics behind Flow Matching models
videos.summary: Julia Turc provides an in-depth analysis of the Flow Matching training algorithm, covering the physics foundations including time-variant probability density, continuity equations, velocity fields, and optimal transport. The video examines Flow Matching alongside concurrent related works including Rectified Flow and Stochastic Interpolants, with detailed treatment of conditional velocity fields and the algorithmic implementation.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Install Claude Code on Mac (EASY)
videos.summary: A tutorial video on installing Claude Code on macOS. Claude Code is presented as a powerful AI coding assistant that provides fine-grained privacy controls over file and folder access, with installation taking less than 2 minutes on Mac.

videos.title: Why You're Still Better Than AI at Editing Documents - CS50 Tech Talk
videos.summary: AI struggles reliably to edit complex Word documents because their internal structure operates like software with its own logic and rules, not like plain text files. Humans excel at single-document editing while maintaining coherence, whereas computers can scale to thousands of documents only if every change is verifiable and trusted. The speaker argues that treating documents as software rather than files is necessary for reliable AI document editing at scale.

videos.title: Most people get these 3 AI terms wrong
videos.summary: Jeff Su argues that AI hallucination is the same mechanism enabling creativity versus inaccuracy, that context window acts like a fixed-size whiteboard with information dropout, and that tokens are discrete chunks of text with associated costs that explain usage limits.

videos.title: Claude Mythos explained..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Anthropic's Claude Mythos as a frontier model representing a significant intelligence leap similar to o1. The video examines private token restrictions and their impact on access, inference costs, pricing models, and broader AI adoption trends.

videos.title: Teaching the foundations of AI in the classroom
videos.summary: Experience AI is a free educational program from Google DeepMind and the Raspberry Pi Foundation designed to teach AI and machine learning foundations to school-aged students. It provides lessons, videos, worksheets, and teacher training using research-backed pedagogies and has reached educators in over 165 countries.

videos.title: Claude Managed Agents Just Dropped, And It Kills n8n
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Managed Agents, a platform designed to replace no-code automation builders like n8n. The service enables users to build autonomous agents without API keys, with features including built-in debugging, session management, environment vaults, security controls, and cost analytics.

videos.title: 5 Claude Cowork Tricks (in 10 min)
videos.summary: A YouTube video tutorial by Tristen O'Brien demonstrates five practical uses of Claude Cowork, an AI tool by Anthropic, for automating business tasks. The video shows how Claude Cowork can reduce a 10-hour daily administrative workload to a streamlined process by analyzing financial spreadsheets, comparing supplier quotes, and generating business reports for small businesses.

videos.title: Claude Mythos Preview: Everything You Need to Know
videos.summary: Nick Saraev reviews Claude Mythos, questioning whether Anthropic released a model "too good" that may signal the end of open Claude models and mark a shift toward a next generation of closed models. The video suggests Mythos demonstrates capabilities that could reshape the landscape of accessible AI models.

videos.title: Learn 80% of Claude Cowork in Under 20 Minutes
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is Anthropic's desktop app that extends Claude from a chatbot into a full productivity system. It enables local file access, persistent memory, connectors, skills, projects, browser extension, and scheduled tasks for automating workflows like expense reports and inbox triage.

videos.title: Mission Report Ep. 03 | Antigravity Updates
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agentic development platform that evolves the IDE into an agent-first era. It enables developers to manage agents across workspaces while retaining a familiar AI IDE experience, with agents operating autonomously across editor, terminal, and browser to plan and execute complex end-to-end tasks.

videos.title: AutoResearch explained..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains AutoResearch from Andrej Karpathy as an early approach to iterative self-improvement in software development. The video discusses how AutoResearch offers a novel perspective on improving software, though notes the approach is not universally applicable.

videos.title: Introducing Cursor 3
videos.summary: Cursor announces version 3 of its AI-powered code editor, emphasizing simplicity and power for a future where agents write code. The release maintains the depth of a development environment while redesigning for agent-driven workflows.

videos.title: #claude Chat vs. Cowork: Biggest 3 differences
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is positioned as an advanced variant of Claude Chat with three key improvements: direct file access from the user's computer without upload limits or file size caps, a larger context window that preserves conversation details longer, and integrated output generation that produces ready-to-use files directly in folders rather than chat-based responses.

videos.title: TurboQuant Explained..
videos.summary: TurboQuant is a Google quantization technique that compresses KV cache and vectors to reduce VRAM and graphics card requirements. It uses vector quantization with codebooks and optimizes for mean squared error and inner product error.

videos.title: I Stopped Typing to OpenClaw — Typeless voice dictation
videos.summary: Typeless is an AI voice dictation tool that enables users to compose and control applications via speech input at significantly higher speeds than typing. The tool supports multilingual content creation, AI-assisted transcription, and integration with other applications through voice-controlled actions.

videos.title: CS50 Outtakes 2013
videos.summary: CS50 Outtakes 2013 is a compilation of out-of-take moments from Harvard's CS50 computer science course. The content provides informal, unscripted commentary on computer science education and instruction.

videos.title: Arm AGI CPU explained..
videos.summary: Arm announced its first AGI CPU targeting agentic workloads, entering a highly competitive CPU market. The market is capital-intensive with high time-to-market risk as hyperscalers increasingly customize CPUs for their specific data center architectures, working with TSMC on supply chain management.

videos.title: How to Use Gamma AI to Make Stunning Presentations
videos.summary: Gamma AI is an AI-powered presentation tool that generates complete presentations from a single prompt, document, or URL in minutes. It automates research, structure, design, and revisions while offering editing via built-in AI agents, presentation modes, and export to PowerPoint, Google Slides, and PDF. The tool includes API capabilities for enterprise automation in sales, marketing, training, and reporting workflows.

videos.title: I Built an AI That Builds You a Course on Anything (In Seconds)
videos.summary: Tristen O'Brien built an AI tool that generates personalized online courses based on user-specified topics and skill levels in under two minutes. The tool structures learning pathways using Claude, organizes content into syllabi, and includes an AI chatbot tutor for course interaction.

videos.title: How to Build Professional Video Animations with Claude.
videos.summary: Tutorial on using Claude Code and Remotion to generate professional video animations from text prompts. Covers installation, visual style configuration, plan mode workflow, and end-to-end animation creation in approximately ten minutes without manual editing.

videos.title: Paperclip Sucks, Actually
videos.summary: Nick Saraev argues that Paperclip is overhyped and encourages looking for better alternatives. He positions himself as someone with practical real-world automation experience, having built automated businesses generating seven-figure revenue over six years.

videos.title: CLAUDE CODE ADVANCED FULL COURSE (3 HOURS)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev teaches an advanced 3-hour course on Claude Code covering system prompts optimization, agent harnesses, multi-agent teams, subagents, auto-research workflows, browser automation, and workspace organization for building larger AI projects.

videos.title: Claude Cowork: The #1 Thing To Know
videos.summary: Claude Cowork differs from Claude Chat in prompting style: Chat requires task-first, step-by-step guidance, while Cowork uses outcome-first language where users describe end goals and let the system determine approach. Example shows Cowork completes photo renaming and sorting autonomously versus Chat providing recommendations for manual execution.

videos.title: OpenClaw Sub Agents and ACPX (OpenClaw Business Automation Setup)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates OpenClaw as a business automation platform deployed on commodity hardware running AI agents to manage websites, learn brand guidelines, and orchestrate sub-agents in content pipelines. The video compares multi-agent orchestration using OpenClaw with single-agent workflows using Claude Code.

videos.title: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Lyria 3 Pro, an advanced music generation model that enables creation of custom tracks up to 3 minutes long with enhanced customization and creative control. The tool is being integrated into more Google products following the earlier release of Lyria 3.

videos.title: Claude Computer Use Just Dropped, Here's How to Hack It
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates practical applications of Claude's computer use feature for automation tasks including LinkedIn outreach, data scraping, form submission, ad management, and QA testing. The video emphasizes real-world revenue-generating use cases and acknowledges some approaches that bypass standard browser features.

videos.title: Qdrant Multi-Vector Search Course Overview
videos.summary: Qdrant has launched a Multi-Vector Search Course available at their course portal. The course covers multi-vector search capabilities, a technique for retrieving information across multiple vector representations simultaneously.

videos.title: Late Interaction Basics | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Late interaction is a search paradigm that preserves token-level representations while enabling pre-computation of documents, contrasted against single-vector embeddings and cross-encoders. The video explains how late interaction models like ColBERT balance retrieval speed with relevance precision for large-scale collections.

videos.title: Use Cases for Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Lesson comparing single-vector and multi-vector search approaches using BGE versus ColBERT on technical support queries. Multi-vector search with token-level verification prevents false positives from keyword-stuffed content by requiring strong matches at the token level rather than just topical overlap.

videos.title: Problems of Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Multi-vector search improves retrieval quality but requires brute-force scanning because HNSW indexing fails with MaxSim due to its asymmetric, query-dependent nature. Storing hundreds of token embeddings per document incurs roughly 167x more memory overhead than single-vector approaches, creating significant computational and storage costs at scale.

videos.title: Multi-Vector Embeddings in Qdrant | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Tutorial on configuring Qdrant for multi-vector search using the MaxSim comparator, indexing documents with FastEmbed local inference, and querying with ColBERT embeddings. Covers collection setup, HNSW disabling for MaxSim compatibility, and on-disk vector storage for memory-constrained environments.

videos.title: How ColPali Models Work | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: ColPali extends late interaction from text to visual documents by using a Vision Language Model (PaliGemma-3B) to divide document images into a 32x32 grid of patches, each producing visual token embeddings. Text queries match directly against these visual patches using MaxSim scoring without requiring OCR.

videos.title: ColPali Family Overview | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: The ColPali family includes compact embedding models (ColSmol, ColFlor, ColModernVBERT) for visual document retrieval with reduced parameters compared to the base ColPali. Multilingual variants from NVIDIA and Nomic AI are available, and ViDoRe V3 serves as the benchmark for evaluating visual retrieval models. Qdrant is a vector search engine in Rust supporting semantic and hybrid search.

videos.title: Visual Interpretability of ColPali | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant demonstrates visual interpretability for ColPali embeddings, which represent documents as 1,024 patch embeddings mapped to spatial locations. The system computes per-token similarity heatmaps overlaid on documents to show which regions match query terms, enabling interpretable multi-vector search and relevance debugging.

videos.title: Multi-Stage Retrieval with Universal Query API | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant's prefetch plus rerank pattern uses fast initial retrieval (single-vector or BM25) to narrow candidates down from millions to hundreds, then applies ColBERT reranking to refine results. The technique emphasizes oversampling in stage one to ensure sufficient candidates for effective reranking.
